theme.title: Object Pronouns in Context
theme.description: B1 practice with direct and indirect object pronouns, contrast, and more natural sentence patterns.

lesson.title: Lesson 9: Longer Context Chains
lesson.instructions: Follow object pronouns across clauses and connected ideas in more advanced B1 sentences.

exercises.prompt: Mi hermana encontro mis audifonos y me los dejo en la mesa para que no los perdiera otra vez.
exercises.answer: My sister found my headphones and left them for me on the table so that I would not lose them again.
exercises.explanation: Track the receiver and the direct object across both clauses.

exercises.prompt: The professor gave us a lot of advice, but we did not know how to apply it to the project.
exercises.answer: El profesor nos dio muchos consejos, pero no sabiamos como aplicarlos al proyecto.
exercises.explanation: The second clause replaces 'consejos' with a direct object pronoun.

exercises.prompt: Si alguien te ofrece una solucion rapida, no la aceptes sin revisarla bien.
exercises.answer: If someone offers you a quick solution, do not accept it without reviewing it well.
exercises.answer_alt: If someone offers you a quick solution, do not accept it without checking it carefully.
exercises.explanation: This sentence chains an indirect object with repeated direct object reference.

exercises.prompt: I wanted to show my grandparents the photos from the trip, but I forgot to send them to them.
exercises.answer: Queria mostrarles a mis abuelos las fotos del viaje, pero olvide mandarselas.
exercises.answer_alt: Queria mostrar las fotos del viaje a mis abuelos, pero olvide mandarselas.
exercises.explanation: Use attached double pronouns in the second clause.

exercises.prompt: Nos lo habian advertido varias veces, pero no quisimos hacerles caso.
exercises.answer: They had warned us about it several times, but we did not want to pay attention to them.
exercises.explanation: This sentence combines 'nos lo' with another indirect object phrase later.

exercises.prompt: When they explained the policy to me, I understood it, but I still did not agree with them.
exercises.answer: Cuando me explicaron la politica, la entendi, pero aun no estaba de acuerdo con ellos.
exercises.explanation: Spanish prefers a natural contrast rather than forcing an extra pronoun in the last clause.

exercises.prompt: Voy a proponerselo manana, y si lo acepta, te lo contare enseguida.
exercises.answer: I am going to propose it to him tomorrow, and if he accepts it, I will tell you about it right away.
exercises.answer_alt: I am going to propose it to her tomorrow, and if she accepts it, I will tell you right away.
exercises.explanation: This sentence contains several pronouns that refer to different ideas across clauses.

exercises.prompt: They did not lend me the tools, so I had to buy them and return them to the store later.
exercises.answer: No me prestaron las herramientas, asi que tuve que comprarlas y devolverlas a la tienda mas tarde.
exercises.explanation: The direct object changes across the sentence but stays consistent in meaning.
